title: Ex-wife of man found dismembered in freezer in Kobe arrested
summary: The woman is suspected of abandoning the body and police are investigating a possible homicide.

title: Japan Supreme Court upholds dissolution of Unification Church
summary: The highest court rejected the sect's appeal and ruled that there was organized involvement in serious financial damages.

title: Japan marks 81st anniversary of Battle of Okinawa amid boos for prime minister
summary: At the ceremony, the governor of Okinawa avoided commenting on peace education and a poem by a student was read.

title: Tokyo school fire forces building demolition and student relocation
summary: Elementary school students will be transferred to neighboring schools while a new school is built on the same site.

title: Japan's emperor and empress begin state visit to Belgium with official reception
summary: The monarchs were received by the Belgian king and queen at the Royal Palace of Brussels and will attend a gala dinner.

title: Fire destroys eight buildings in Izumo shopping district, Shimane Prefecture
summary: No casualties have been reported, but firefighters continue working to extinguish the flames in the city center area.

title: Ishin party agrees to amend vice capital bill at prime minister's request
summary: The agreement allows progress in parliamentary proceedings, while the administrative reform of Osaka is debated.

title: Japanese economic mission meets Chinese authorities to improve bilateral ties
summary: Lawmaker Hashimoto and other business leaders seek to resume dialogue following tensions triggered by Prime Minister Takaisi's remarks.

title: Japanese stock markets plunge, Nikkei falls over 3%
summary: The benchmark index lost 2,565 points and fell below 70,000 yen, dragged down by massive selling in the tech sector.

title: Yen weakens to 161.5 against dollar, government threatens intervention
summary: The Japanese currency approaches its lowest level in 39 years, leading the finance minister to announce measures together with the United States.

title: NHK posts deficits for seven straight years, considers raising license fee
summary: The public broadcaster posted a deficit of 31.8 billion yen in the last fiscal year and license fee income continues to decline.

title: Most accounting fraud charges against LDP lawmaker dismissed
summary: The court acquitted Ono of several document falsification charges in the undeclared funds case, although a partial sentence was imposed.

title: Nissan shareholders reject reelection of independent director Nagai
summary: The vote at the general meeting reflects concern over the board's lack of independence and dissatisfaction with corporate governance.

title: Toki Air CEO dismissed after one year in position
summary: The airline, facing financial troubles, replaced its top executive at the shareholders' meeting, but founder Horie retains his position.

title: Two former Tokyo highway employees disciplined for rigging bids
summary: The investigation revealed a bid-rigging scheme in Shuto Expressway cleaning contracts that led to disciplinary measures.

title: China reclaims world's top supercomputing spot ahead of US
summary: The Asian country returns to the number one spot in the ranking after nine years, with systems that had not been previously revealed.

title: Cool Japan agency accumulates losses of over 500 billion yen
summary: The Japanese government is considering abolishing the public body dedicated to promoting popular culture due to its continuous financial troubles.

title: Trump claims Iran accepted nuclear inspections, but Persian regime denies it
summary: The US granted a 60-day license for Iranian crude oil exports, amid the diplomatic conflict over the nuclear program.

title: Courtroom intrusion disrupts sentencing for murder of student in Hokkaido
summary: The woman was sentenced to 27 years in prison, but the proceeding was disrupted by a person who entered the courtroom during the reading of the verdict.

title: Heavy rain alert and risk of linear precipitation bands in northern Kyushu
summary: Meteorological authorities warn of possible flooding and river overflows until Tuesday, especially in Nagasaki and Kumamoto.
